# UC-22.2.14 · NIS2 Human Resources Security — Joiner/Mover/Leaver Process

## Description

Joins authoritative HR termination dates from `terminated_employees.csv` (or HEC `hr:jml_events`) against subsequent authentication activity drawn from Entra ID, Windows, and shared auth indexes to spotlight accounts still authenticating after the leaver timestamp.

## Value

Provisioning audits rarely fail on paper—they fail when old accounts keep logging in because a deletion script missed a shadow admin; giving the privacy officer and risk committee a dated trail of post-termination authentications closes that evidentiary gap ahead of regulator questions.

## Implementation

(1) HR publishes `terminated_employees.csv` nightly with `user` UPN + `termination_date`. (2) Map `index=hr` feed for near-real-time events. (3) Alert if `days_active_after_term>0`. (4) Severity++ if >3 days. (5) Auto-task ServiceNow `RITM` via alert. (6) Store signed exports with `evidenceSigning`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Authoritative HR feed: either CSV (`terminated_employees.csv`) dropped into `lookups/` with ACL `hr_compliance` read-only, or HEC to `index=hr` `sourcetype=hr:jml_events` containing `termination_date`, `employee_id`, `user`, `event_type`.
• Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services ([Splunkbase 3110](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3110)) ensuring Entra sign-ins include `userPrincipalName` for correlation.
• Splunk Add-on for Windows ([Splunkbase 742](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)) for Domain Controller `WinEventLog:Security` success codes 4624/4768.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) optional for validating `RITM` closure states.
• Legal basis documented: DPIA stating Splunk processes HR identifiers under Art.6(1)(c) GDPR.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• HR job writes CSV with columns `user,termination_date,reason_code` daily at 01:00 local; Splunk heavy forwarder rsync pulls to `lookups/` then `splunk reload lookup-table` via REST.
• For HEC, props.conf sets `TIME_FORMAT=%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z` on `hr:jml_events`.
• Validate counts: `| inputlookup terminated_employees.csv | stats count` matches Workday export row totals.
• ServiceNow TA: enable `snow:task` input filtering `short_description=*depropvision*` (tune query).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Use `spl` field; add `| where days_active_after_term>0` alert daily 06:00.
Alternate streaming variant replacing CSV with `index=hr event_type=termination` generating identical fields via `rename`.

Step 3 — Validate
• For synthetic user, disable account in AD and confirm final Windows logon timestamp aligns (+/−5 min) with Splunk `last_auth`.
• Cross-check Azure portal **Revoke sessions** action timestamps.
• Legal sample: request random ex-employee folder — compare Splunk evidence CSV with HR PDF letter date.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• Dashboard with red/yellow thresholds (0 days = info, ≥3 days = critical).
• Integrate SOAR to call **Microsoft Graph /users/{id}/invalidateAllRefreshTokens** (external runbook).
• Quarterly evidence pack signer references `evidenceSigning` manifest.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **join drops everything** — field case mismatch; enforce lower(`user`).
• **Mass false positives after domain migration** — refresh CSV UPNs.
• **subsearch limits** — switch to `| map` or summary index `summary_jml`.
• **Azure misses last_auth** — verify `index=azure` alias; add `index=auth` to subsearch.


## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup terminated_employees.csv
| eval term_date=strptime(termination_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_since_term=round((now()-term_date)/86400, 0)
| join type=left user [
    search index=auth OR index=windows OR index=azure earliest=-30d
    | eval user=coalesce(Account_Name, userPrincipalName, user)
    | stats latest(_time) as last_auth count as auth_count by user
]
| where isnotnull(last_auth) AND last_auth > term_date
| eval days_active_after_term=round((last_auth - term_date)/86400, 0)
| sort - days_active_after_term
| table user, termination_date, days_since_term, days_active_after_term, auth_count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user
```

## Visualization

Timeline of last_auth vs term_date, gauge of open violations, ServiceNow ticket drilldown.
